issue_title: Default local model: introduce `genie-ai-model` concept with Qwen 3 4B paired with genie-ai-runtime
issue_body:
## Background

The bundled local model story today is implicit and Phi-4-mini-shaped:

| Where | What |
|---|---|
| `deploy/config/geniepod.toml:10` | `llm_model_name = "phi"` (drives prompt-template selection in `genie-core`) |
| `deploy/config/geniepod.toml:96` | `llm_model_path = "/opt/geniepod/models/phi-4-mini-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf"` |
| `deploy/systemd/genie-llm.service:30` | `Environment=GENIEPOD_LLM_MODEL=/opt/geniepod/models/phi-4-mini-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf` |
| `deploy/setup-jetson.sh:66-85` | downloads `Phi-4-mini-instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf` from `lmstudio-community/Phi-4-mini-instruct-GGUF` |

So "the default model" is encoded in three places, fetched from one source, and named by one bespoke string (`"phi"`) in the prompt-builder switch (`crates/genie-core/src/prompt.rs`).

That's a coordination tax every time the default changes. It also has no shared identity — `genie-ai-runtime` validated against `Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf` specifically (see their README), and the runtime's claimed **+115% prefill / −61% TTFT** numbers are against *that* model. The deploy story doesn't reflect that pairing.

## Proposal

Introduce a `genie-ai-model` concept — the canonical "default bundled model" layer — and default it to **Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf**, paired with `genie-ai-runtime` (which is itself the proposed default in #33).

The concept can be realized two ways:

**Option A — single source of truth in genie-claw** (lighter, recommended for the first iteration):

- A new `deploy/models/MODELS.toml` (or top-level `models/default.toml`) that names the default model with metadata: name, repo source, expected SHA256, recommended runtime, prompt-template family.
- `setup-jetson.sh` reads it, downloads accordingly.
- `geniepod.toml` references it by alias rather than absolute path.
- `genie-llm.service` and `genie-ai-runtime.service` derive their `GENIEPOD_LLM_MODEL` from the same source.
- One PR-sized change.

**Option B — separate `GeniePod/genie-ai-model` repo** (heavier, deferred until multi-model management becomes a real concern):

- A new repo holding model manifests (no weights — GGUF binaries stay on Hugging Face).
- `make download` helper that resolves the manifest into local files.
- Versioned releases that pin a default model per release tag.
- Genie-claw becomes a consumer that fetches the manifest at `setup-jetson.sh` time.

**Recommendation: start with Option A.** Genie-claw is the only consumer today; the manifest can stay in-tree. If `genie-os`, `genie-ai-runtime`, or a future installer ever needs to consume the same default model selection, extract to Option B as a follow-up.

## Why Qwen 3 4B specifically

Comparing against the current Phi-4-mini default on the relevant axes for genie-claw's household-AI use case:

| Axis | Phi-4-mini (Q4_K_M, ~2.4 GB) | Qwen 3 4B (Q4_K_M, ~2.5 GB) |
|---|---|---|
| **Context window (native)** | 128K | 32K (extends to 128K with YaRN) |
| **Reasoning** | Solid for size | Stronger; explicit "thinking" mode option |
| **Tool-call instruction following** | Acceptable; needs prompt-shape coaxing | Tuned for structured output; cleaner JSON tool calls (relevant to `crates/genie-core/src/tools/dispatch.rs` parser) |
| **Multilingual** | English-dominant | Strong multilingual, including East-Asian languages — matches the `voice/language.rs` per-language Piper voice-selection feature |
| **Latency on llama.cpp + Orin Nano** | ~good | ~slower per-token (longer attention) |
| **Latency on genie-ai-runtime + Orin Nano** | n/a (not validated) | **Validated by the runtime team; the +115% prefill / −61% TTFT numbers cited in genie-ai-runtime README are against this model** |

The weakness of Qwen 3 4B on the current llama.cpp default — slower decode than Phi-4-mini — is exactly what `genie-ai-runtime` was built to address. The two together are a Pareto improvement on reasoning quality without losing voice-cycle latency. Either alone is a regression on one axis.

That's the coupling argument: **Qwen 3 4B should land as the default only in combination with `genie-ai-runtime` as the default backend**. If genie-claw flips the model default first (on llama.cpp), voice cycles regress. If genie-claw flips the runtime default first (#33) but keeps Phi-4-mini, the runtime's headline numbers don't materialize (they were measured against Qwen 3 4B).

## Implementation sketch (Option A)

```
deploy/models/MODELS.toml          (new — manifest)
deploy/setup-jetson.sh             (read manifest, download + verify SHA)
deploy/config/geniepod.toml        ([core] llm_model_name + llm_model_path from manifest)
deploy/systemd/genie-llm.service           (`GENIEPOD_LLM_MODEL` derived)
deploy/systemd/genie-ai-runtime.service    (per #33; same derivation)
crates/genie-core/src/prompt.rs    (already has the `"qwen"` family — verify the Qwen 3 template works as-is or extend)
```

Manifest shape:

```toml
[default]
alias = "qwen3-4b-q4_k_m"
file = "qwen3-4b-q4_k_m.gguf"
source = "huggingface://Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-GGUF/qwen3-4b-q4_k_m.gguf"
sha256 = "..."                       # pinned per release
size_bytes = 2_500_000_000            # approx
prompt_family = "qwen"                # matches CoreConfig::llm_model_name
recommended_backend = "genie_ai_runtime"
context_size = 32768

[alternate.phi-4-mini]
alias = "phi-4-mini-q4_k_m"
file = "phi-4-mini-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf"
source = "huggingface://lmstudio-community/Phi-4-mini-instruct-GGUF/Phi-4-mini-instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf"
sha256 = "..."
prompt_family = "phi"
recommended_backend = "llama_cpp"
```

A `[default]` swap is then a one-line manifest change.

## Phase split

**Phase 1 (this issue's MVP, opt-in):**

- Add Qwen 3 4B as a *selectable* model: extend `setup-jetson.sh` with a `--model qwen3-4b` flag (default still `phi-4-mini`), document the combo with `genie-ai-runtime` in README / GETTING_STARTED, no default change.
- Add a regression test against the `qwen` prompt-template path in `prompt.rs` (today the switch has `"qwen"` as an option but it's not the default — verify it covers Qwen 3's chat template, not just Qwen 2).

**Phase 2 (couples with #33):**

- Introduce the `genie-ai-model` manifest layer (Option A).
- Flip default to Qwen 3 4B *in the same commit that flips* `[services.llm].backend` default to `genie_ai_runtime` per #33.
- Land the deploy + setup-jetson + systemd unit updates atomically.
- Parity gate from #33 extends to cover the voice-cycle latency with the new model+runtime pair on real Orin Nano hardware.

**Phase 3 (future, only if needed):**

- Extract to a `GeniePod/genie-ai-model` repo with versioned manifest releases.
- Multi-model registry (the `[alternate.*]` table above grows).

## Acceptance criteria — Phase 1

- [ ] `deploy/setup-jetson.sh --model qwen3-4b` downloads `Qwen3-4B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf` to `/opt/geniepod/models/`.
- [ ] `deploy/config/geniepod.toml` documents (commented) the `llm_model_name = "qwen"` + `llm_model_path = "/opt/geniepod/models/qwen3-4b-q4_k_m.gguf"` pair.
- [ ] `crates/genie-core/src/prompt.rs`'s `"qwen"` family is verified against Qwen 3's actual chat template; regression test added.
- [ ] README / GETTING_STARTED gains a one-paragraph "Recommended for low-latency reasoning: Qwen 3 4B + `genie-ai-runtime`" section pointing at the combo.
- [ ] No default change. Existing Phi-4-mini deployments keep working.

## Acceptance criteria — Phase 2 (lands with #33)

- [ ] `deploy/models/MODELS.toml` exists, names Qwen 3 4B as `[default]`.
- [ ] `setup-jetson.sh` reads the manifest; the legacy `--model` flag still works for one release as a deprecation path.
- [ ] `geniepod.toml` and both systemd units derive `GENIEPOD_LLM_MODEL` from the manifest (or are kept hand-edited but documented as such).
- [ ] Voice-cycle p50/p95 on Orin Nano with the new model+runtime combo is within 30% of the current Phi-4-mini + llama.cpp baseline (better is the expectation; this is the regression gate).
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry under alpha.8 (or whichever release flips the default).
- [ ] Rollback story documented — switching `[default]` in the manifest back to `phi-4-mini` + setting `[services.llm].backend = "llama_cpp"` reverts both changes atomically.

## Non-goals

- **No multi-model concurrency**, no model-router, no per-conversation model selection. One default at a time.
- **No GGUF distribution from GeniePod infrastructure.** Models stay on Hugging Face; the manifest is metadata only.
- **No new fine-tuned variants.** Defaulting to upstream Qwen 3 4B Instruct as-released, not a household-tuned fork.
- **No change to Phi-4-mini availability** — it stays selectable as an alternate. The point is to flip the recommended default, not to remove a working option.

## Risk

- **Phase 1 is low-risk.** No default change; opt-in via flag. Hard to break.
- **Phase 2 risk = #33's risk.** Anyone upgrading from a current alpha to the alpha that flips the default needs to know `setup-jetson.sh` will fetch a different model and the systemd units point elsewhere. CHANGELOG + a `setup-jetson.sh` warning for "you have phi-4-mini installed; default is now qwen3-4b, run with `--model phi-4-mini` to keep the old default" handles the upgrade surprise.

## Related

- **#32** (LLM backend abstraction) — closed; provides the `[services.llm].backend` switch this proposal relies on.
- **#33** (default-flip to `genie-ai-runtime`) — this issue's Phase 2 should land in the same commit as #33's default flip. The two are tightly coupled: Qwen 3 4B without `genie-ai-runtime` is a latency regression; `genie-ai-runtime` without Qwen 3 4B doesn't realize the runtime's headline TTFT numbers.
- **#41** (voice as Cargo feature) — independent; not affected.
- **`GeniePod/genie-ai-runtime`** (upstream) — validated against Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf per their README. This proposal closes the loop on that pairing in the consuming repo.

## Why now

- Five abstraction slices (#35, #38, #39, #40, #43) just landed making backend selection real.
- #33 is the next architectural milestone — flipping the runtime default.
- Flipping the runtime default without flipping the model default leaves the runtime's measured advantage on the table.
- Phase 1 is small enough to land as a single PR right now; Phase 2 sequences naturally behind #33.
